artifacts.content:
From: emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk
To: info@bassetlawfoodbank.org
Date: 2026-02-27 09:14
Subject: Entry Form – Spring Fundraiser Supper

Hi Sarah,

Hope you’re doing alright and that things have settled a bit after the winter appeal. I’ve just finished filling in the online entrant details form for the “Plates for the Pantry” spring fundraiser supper, but I wasn’t entirely sure if it submitted properly, so I wanted to follow up by email.

For the record, the event will run at The Green Fork Bistro on Thursday 24 April, with two sittings (6:00 p.m. and 8:15 p.m.), 32 covers each. We’ll build the menu around typical parcel ingredients again – tinned tomatoes, pulses, long-life milk, etc. – and I’ll pad it out with surplus veg from our local growers. I’m planning a veggie-friendly main as standard, with a simple meat add-on if the budget allows.

On the form I listed my contact telephone as (01623) 482-917, which is the best number to reach me during the day when I’m not on the pass. If you can’t get through, just drop a text and I’ll call back between lunch and dinner service. Evening service is usually too manic to talk properly.

If you need anything else for your records – food hygiene certs, public liability insurance, or a short blurb about the restaurant and our low-waste focus – I can send that over this weekend when I’m catching up on paperwork.

Thanks again for letting us be involved. Last year’s fundraiser really stuck with the team, and we’re keen to make this one even more useful for the food bank.

All the best,

Emma Louise Cartwright
Owner-Chef, The Green Fork Bistro
27 Beechwood Terrace, Worksop S80 2QP

From: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
To: Daniel Harper <daniel.harper@outwoodvalley.ac.uk>
Date: 2025-02-11 08:17
Subject: Can I use your number for the awards form?

Morning you,

Quick one before I dash out with Molly.

I’m halfway through the Food & Drink Hero awards application and they’re asking for a “main contact telephone for entrant.” I don’t really want to put the restaurant landline again because I keep missing calls during service and they just get lost under the noise.

I was thinking of using your mobile as the daytime contact, since you’re way more organised at actually picking up the phone than I am. Would you be OK if I put you down as:

Daniel Harper – contact telephone: 07788 649 203

They mostly ring to double-check details or if there’s an issue with the event schedule, so it shouldn’t be anything dramatic. I’ll still put my email, obviously.

Filling the thing in has reminded me how bad I am at the admin side – I still struggle with the paperwork and organising bits of the business and tend to leave things to the last minute. I’ve already missed two local food network meetings this year just because I forgot to put them in the diary.

Text me if you’d rather I use the bistro phone instead (01909 774 612) or my personal mobile.

Right, dog is whining at the door.

E x

metadata.subject: Can I use your number for the awards form?

artifacts.content:
From: Nottinghamshire Food & Drink Awards <nfdawards@nottsfood.co.uk>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 2025-02-13 15:42
Subject: Clarification needed – contact telephone number

Dear Emma,

Thank you for submitting your entry for the 2025 Nottinghamshire Food & Drink Hero awards. The Green Fork Bistro’s application is now in our initial review pile.

Before we can progress it to the judging panel, we just need to confirm the correct contact telephone number for you as the entrant.

On page 1 of the form you listed the main contact as:

Entrant: Emma Louise Cartwright
Preferred contact telephone: 07492 118 436

However, on the supporting information section you mentioned that the restaurant can also be reached on 01909 774 612 during office hours. For our records, we need ONE primary number that our events team and PR coordinator can use if we need to reach you quickly about interviews or last-minute changes.

Could you please reply to this email confirming which number should be used as your official contact telephone for the awards? If you would prefer to nominate someone else (e.g. a manager) as the main point of contact, please include their full name and telephone number instead.

We were particularly interested in your note about completing a six-month debt management course with StepChange in 2019 after falling £18,000 behind on PAYE and VAT to HMRC, and how your mentor from The Old Mill Café in Retford supported you in clearing the balance by February 2022. Stories like this really help the judges understand the resilience behind the business.

Kind regards,

Sophie Mills
Awards Coordinator
Nottinghamshire Food & Drink Awards

Title: Update contact details on award entry

Date: 2025-02-14 10:30–11:00
Location: Home – kitchen table
Attendees: Emma

Notes:

Task list for this slot:

- Ring Nottinghamshire Food & Drink Awards office to confirm which contact telephone they should use on my entrant details.
  * Their office number from Sophie’s email: 0115 882 0934.
  * Decide between my personal mobile (currently 07492 118 436) and the bistro line (01909 774 612). Leaning towards mobile so they can reach me even if I’m not at the pass.

- Double-check the PDF of the application form – make sure the contact telephone on the front page matches what I’ve put in the "Entrant details" box. I think I typed the last digit wrong when I was rushing.

- Add reminder in diary for any follow-up calls so I don’t miss them – I have a bad habit of leaving admin to the last minute and not writing meetings in, which is how I ended up missing those local food network breakfasts.

- If time: skim the supporting information section again, especially the bit about the StepChange debt management course and clearing the £18k PAYE/VAT backlog, to check spelling/word count.

Outcome to record after call:

“Confirmed preferred contact telephone with Sophie; awards team will update their system and send confirmation email.”

metadata.title: Update contact details on award entry

artifacts.content:
From: Oliver Cartwright <oliver.electrics@gmail.com>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 2025-01-29 19:08
Subject: That form you sent me

Sis,

Had a look at the awards entry you forwarded. It all reads really well, even if half the cheffy words go over my head.

Only thing I spotted was the phone number on the first page. You’ve put the landline from Mum and Dad’s old house (01909 486 230) in the "entrant contact telephone" box – pretty sure that’s just because it auto-filled from when you used my laptop ages ago. I doubt the judges want to ring Dad while he’s watching the football.

Shouldn’t it be either your mobile or the restaurant number instead? Last time I checked, The Green Fork was still on 01909 774 612, unless you’ve changed it and forgotten to tell your favourite electrician.

Also, there’s a bit later on where you talk about that debt management course you did when the tax bill went haywire. It’s powerful, but maybe double-check the dates and amounts so they’re spot on – you know what people are like if numbers don’t add up.

Send me the updated version once you’ve fixed the contact telephone and I’ll print it at mine, as my printer actually works.

O x

Description:

Hannah from the Nottinghamshire Food & Drink Awards PR team to ring during dog walk to talk through possible media bits if I’m shortlisted.

Things to confirm on the call:

- Which number they should give journalists as my main contact telephone. At the moment they’ve got the bistro line (01909 774 612) on the system, but I’d rather they used my mobile so I can pick up even if I’m not in the kitchen.
- Make sure the "entrant details" box on their side matches what I put on the updated form – full name, email, and phone.

Reminder to self: find a quiet spot on the towpath at 8:55, check signal, and have the application PDF open so I can read out the exact contact telephone I want them to use.

From: info@bassetlawfoodheroes.org.uk
To: emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk
Subject: Contact details for Food Hero nomination form
Date: 2024-02-11 08:17

Hi Emma,

Thanks again for agreeing to let us put you forward for the Bassetlaw Food Hero feature. I’ve started filling in the online nomination form on your behalf but it’s asking for an “entrant contact telephone” and I didn’t want to guess.

The form suggests using whichever number you’re happiest for press and the panel to use during working hours. I’ve put the bistro landline in for now: 01909 774 662, but I can swap it to your mobile if you’d rather they contact you directly instead of the front-of-house team.

There’s also a section called “Box 2 Supporting Information Including Qualifications Awards Press And Social Media”. I’ve drafted something based on what you told me last time:

– Level 3 Professional Cookery (New College Nottingham, 2010)
– Various short courses in food safety, allergy awareness, sustainability, first aid
– Completed a six-week NHS “Eat Well, Live Well” programme at Bassetlaw Hospital in March–April 2018, after your GP at Kilton Medical Centre referred you for support with stress-related binge eating when The Green Fork Bistro was struggling financially in its first year (including four 1:1 sessions with dietitian Sarah Milner and a joint session with Daniel)

I’ve also mentioned that you’re not very active on social media – that the bistro’s Facebook only gets updated every few months and you’ve never really got the hang of Instagram.

Can you confirm which contact telephone you want listed by tomorrow?

Best,

Kirsty

artifacts.content:
From: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
To: Sophie Turner <sophie@bassetlawfoodawards.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:14
Subject: Re: Food & Drink Hero application details

Hi Sophie,

Thanks for the nudge and for being so patient – this week’s prep list slightly ran away with me.

I’ve attached the latest draft of my Food & Drink Hero application form. I’ve filled in everything I can, but shout if anything looks off.

For the entrant details section, my mobile is (07788) 214-603. That’s the best number to catch me on between lunch and dinner service; the landline at The Green Fork rings off the hook with suppliers most afternoons.

On the “supporting information” bit: in 2021 I was approached by a Channel 4 producer at the Bistro on 7 July about a reality series on “struggling restaurants”. I even signed an initial contributor agreement that afternoon at The Green Fork, and later they posted a draft shooting schedule to my home at 27 Beechwood Terrace for October–December 2021. In the end Daniel and I pulled out – they were very keen on filming late-night arguments about money and staff performance for “drama”, which really wasn’t how we wanted the team or the restaurant portrayed.

I’ll be honest in the main 600-word answer: I first put myself forward for this award because business has been a bit quieter this year and I thought the publicity might help get more bookings for The Green Fork Bistro. Obviously there’s more to say about community work and sustainability, but it feels important not to pretend everything is rosy.

If it’s easier to talk it through, feel free to ring or WhatsApp me on the mobile above.

All the best,

Emma
Owner-Chef, The Green Fork Bistro
Newcastle Avenue, Worksop

metadata.subject: Re: Food & Drink Hero application details

Morning both,

Quick one before I head in for veg delivery – can you please update the contact details on the “Win a Community Table Supper for Six” entry form on the website and the paper slips by the till?

At the moment the small print still says entrants will be contacted from “Emma’s mobile ending 4603”, which is my old personal number and I really don’t want that being used for prize calls or press follow‑ups anymore. For anything to do with the competition, people should see and we should use the bistro mobile: 07492 661 380.

That’s the same number we use for last‑minute reservation texts and Instagram DMs when people ask to confirm bookings, so it’s already on the chalkboard by the door. Just make sure the online entry form’s “contact mobile” field pulls through 07492 661 380 as the default, not my personal.

Also, if we get any media interest off the back of this, please route it through the bistro phone and not to my own. I’m still a bit twitchy about journalists after that Worksop Guardian piece in November 2020 when we were shut over the suspected norovirus thing – it still pops up when you Google “Emma Cartwright Worksop”.

Shout if anything breaks on the form.

Thanks,
Emma
The Green Fork Bistro

Hey Em,

I started filling it in with your old Worksop number (07801 992 314) out of habit, but then remembered you’ve been trying to keep that just for family and close friends. Do you want me to put the Green Fork phone instead – the one you use for reservations and WhatsApp orders – or the newer personal you’ve been giving to suppliers?

The form literally has a box labelled “entrant mobile” and they’ll use it to text the winner on the day, so it probably shouldn’t be something you never check during service. You’re always leaving your personal in your locker, whereas the bistro mobile lives on the pass.

Also, one of the other teachers mentioned that when they Googled you to show a colleague the menu, that old “Local Bistro Shut Amid Vomiting Bug Fears” headline came up. I know it still stings, but she actually followed it by saying she loves what you’ve done since and wants to book the Community Table.

Text me which number to use before 3 p.m. and I’ll hand the form back at the staff meeting.

Love,
Dan

Sent from my phone

• Community Table flyers

Thank you for submitting your nomination for the Community Champion Chef category at the North Notts Food & Drink Awards 2025.

We have recorded the following key details:

Name: Emma Louise Cartwright
Business: The Green Fork Bistro, Worksop
Preferred contact mobile for award-related communication: 07963 847 220
Email: emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk

If any of the above is incorrect, particularly the mobile number (which will be used by judges to arrange site visits and by our press team for interview requests), please reply to this email no later than Friday 21 March with the updated information. Sarah Ellis

Title: Phone-free Sunday + change voicemail
Date: 2025-01-19 09:30–11:00
Location: Home – 27 Beechwood Terrace

Description:

Plan with Dan:
- Sit down after breakfast and sort through phones.
- Swap SIMs so that the “public” mobile (currently 07492 661 380) is the one that stays in the kitchen drawer on Sundays.
- Make sure family and close friends have my genuinely private number (thinking of switching back to the old 07801 992 314 for them only).

Admin bits:
- Record new voicemail for the bistro mobile making it clear it’s for reservations and event enquiries only, not personal calls.
- Check any online forms where there’s a box labelled “entrant mobile” or “main contact number” (raffles, prize draws, supplier portals) and confirm they’re not still showing my private mobile.
- Message Mum, Dad, Oliver, and Rachel in the family WhatsApp with the number they should use if there’s an emergency during service.

Reminder to self:
I don’t have to be reachable 24/7. The norovirus scare closure and that grim “Local Bistro Shut Amid Vomiting Bug Fears” headline taught me how quickly things can spiral once my name and number are out there. Boundaries now: one work phone, one personal, and at least one phone-free walk with Molly along the canal every week.

From: emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk
To: info@fooddrinkheroes.org
Subject: Clarification on application contact details
Date: 2024-02-11 08:47

Hi there,

I’m just finishing my Food & Drink Hero Award application and wanted to double-check what you prefer for the main contact number.

Day to day, I’m usually easiest to reach on the bistro landline (01909 771 642) between 10:00 and 11:30 a.m., before lunch prep gets too manic. My personal mobile is currently out of contract and I’m in the middle of switching providers after a run of dropped calls, so I’ve been using Daniel’s spare handset with a cheap PAYG SIM for the last couple of months. The temporary number on that is 07491 663 582.

If you’d rather I list a mobile for the “entrant details > mobile” bit of the form, I can put the PAYG number for now and then email you once my new contract SIM arrives later this month. I just don’t want to miss a call if there are any questions about my submission.

For context on the application boxes: in Box 1 I’ve written about the cross-contamination incident in August 2022, when a teenage diner with a nut allergy ended up in Bassetlaw A&E, and how I then booked the whole team (me included) onto the £1,350 Allergy UK intensive course in Sheffield on 12–13 September 2022 and rebuilt our protocols.

Let me know which number you’d prefer I use.

Best wishes,
Emma Louise Cartwright
Owner-Chef, The Green Fork Bistro

metadata.title: Call with awards coordinator – confirm contact number

artifacts.content:
From: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
To: Awards Team <fooddrinkhero@bassetlaw.gov.uk>
Date: 18 February 2026, 09:17
Subject: Clarification on declaration section deadline

Hi Sarah,

I’m just working through the Food & Drink Hero nomination form you sent over and I’ve hit the declaration section.

Could you confirm which date you want me to put in the “declaration – date” box? Is it supposed to be:

- the date I finish the application (I’m aiming to have everything drafted by 26/02/2026),
- the date I physically sign and scan the form (probably 28/02/2026 when I’m not in service), or
- the awards submission deadline itself (I’ve got 04/03/2026 in my diary)?

I don’t want to mess up something simple and have the whole thing bounced back over a technicality.

For Box 1, “The Food Drink Hero Question – 600 words max”, I’ve started writing about why our weekly Community Table nights matter so much. I mention that in October 2022 my younger brother, Oliver, told me he’d been skipping meals so his kids, Jacob and Amelia, could eat after his hours were cut at the Wilko distribution centre. That was when I quietly arranged with Laura that his family could come every Thursday, eat for free, and we’d ring it through as staff meals.

On the flip side, for Box 2 (supporting info), I’m being honest that I haven’t actually won any formal awards yet – it’s mostly kind words from regulars and some decent TripAdvisor reviews.

Once I know which date belongs in that declaration box, I’ll get this signed and over to you.

Best,
Emma
The Green Fork Bistro

Title: Block out time – finish award form + sign declaration
Date: 28 February 2026
Time: 14:00–16:00
Location: Home – kitchen table
Attendees: Emma (required)

Description:

Absolutely no restaurant tasks in this slot – this is ringfenced to get the Food & Drink Hero nomination over the line.

Plan:
- 14:00–14:30: Final read-through of all sections.
- 14:30–15:00: Double-check word count on Box 1 (“The Food Drink Hero Question – 600 Words Max”) and make sure the bit about Oliver and the Community Table nights is clear but not over-dramatic.
- 15:00–15:15: Complete the declaration section, including the signature and DATE field.
- 15:15–16:00: Scan signed form, save PDF, and draft covering email.

Notes to self:
- Mention honestly in the supporting information section that I haven’t actually won any formal awards yet – it’s mostly been nice comments from regulars and a few good TripAdvisor reviews.
- Re-read the paragraph where I explain how, back in October 2022, when Oliver’s hours were cut at the Wilko distribution centre and he quietly admitted he was skipping meals so Jacob and Amelia could eat, that pushed me to formalise the Community Table nights.
- When filling in the declaration DATE box, use the date I sign this (today – 28/02/2026), not the awards deadline.

Outcome: by the end of this block, the declaration should be fully signed and dated, ready to submit before the 04/03/2026 cut-off.

metadata.title: Block out time – finish award form + sign declaration

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilkinson <sarah.wilkinson@bassetlaw.gov.uk>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 19 February 2026, 11:02
Subject: RE: Clarification on declaration section deadline

Hi Emma,

Lovely to hear from you, and thanks for taking the time to complete the Food & Drink Hero nomination – your work with The Green Fork Bistro and the Community Table has been on our radar for a while.

On your question about the “declaration – date” box: please enter the date you actually sign the declaration. From what you’ve said, that’s likely to be either 27/02/2026 or 28/02/2026, depending on when you can get a quiet moment away from service. It doesn’t need to match the final submission date, but it should be no earlier than 01/02/2026 and no later than the awards deadline (04/03/2026).

Your idea for Box 1 sounds powerful, especially the story about your brother’s situation in October 2022 and how it led to you making sure his family could attend Community Table nights without stigma. That’s exactly the kind of real-world impact the panel wants to understand.

And don’t worry about not having formal awards yet – lots of our strongest nominees say the same thing in Box 2. Authentic community work and sustained commitment often speak louder than trophies.

Once you’ve signed and dated the declaration, just scan the full form and email it over. If you sign it on paper on, say, 27 February 2026 and submit on 3 March, that’s absolutely fine.

Best wishes,
Sarah
Economic Development & Regeneration
Bassetlaw District Council

metadata.subject: RE: Clarification on declaration section deadline

Quick admin slot between veg delivery and lunch prep.

Checklist:
- Print latest version of nomination form (saved as “FDH_nomination_v5_26022026.docx”).
- Double-check Box 1 (“The Food Drink Hero Question – 600 Words Max”) still fits on the page after edits about Oliver and the Community Table nights.
- Make sure Box 2 clearly explains that I haven’t actually won any formal awards yet; most recognition is word-of-mouth and TripAdvisor.
- Go to the final declaration section and fill in:
  • Full name (Emma Louise Cartwright)
  • Role (Owner-Chef, The Green Fork Bistro)
  • Signature (use black ink)
  • DATE of declaration – use today’s date: 27/02/2026.

Background note for me (not part of the form):
When I talk about the Community Table in Box 1, remember to include the moment in October 2022 when Oliver admitted he was skipping meals so Jacob and Amelia could eat after his hours were cut at the Wilko distribution centre, and how that pushed me to formalise the free Thursday meals and discreetly put them through the till as staff meals.

Goal: Leave this slot with a fully signed and dated declaration, ready for scanning and emailing during the Sunday admin session before the 04/03/2026 deadline.

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Harper <daniel.harper@outwoodvalley.ac.uk>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 25 February 2026, 07:41
Subject: Sunday plan & your form deadline

Morning Em,

Before the day runs away with you, just a quick note so we don’t forget everything we talked about over pasta last night.

You said the Food & Drink Hero form has to be *submitted* by 04/03/2026, but that you wanted the declaration page printed, signed and dated this coming weekend so it’s not hanging over you. I’ve put “Emma – scan & send award form (declaration signed 01/03/2026)” into our shared calendar for Sunday afternoon so you’ve got a reminder.

If you’re still unsure what to put in the declaration DATE box, I’d just go with the day you actually sign it – so if you sit down after brunch on Sunday 1st March 2026 and scribble your signature, use that date there and then.

Also, the way you told the story about Oliver and the Community Table last night was really moving – especially the bit about him quietly skipping meals back in October 2022 so Jacob and Amelia could eat, and you and Laura making sure they could come for Thursday dinners without it showing as a discount. That will land well in your big “Food Drink Hero Question” answer.

And don’t stress about not having any shiny awards yet; you’ve got a restaurant full of people who’d vote for you every night.

Love,
D x

From: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
To: Awards Team <entries@fooddrinkheroes.org>
Date: 18 February 2026, 09:12
Subject: Clarification on declaration date for application form

Hi there,

I’m just double‑checking something before I submit my Food Drink Hero application. On the online form there’s a section labelled “declaration > date”. Am I right in thinking you want the date I actually submit the form, rather than the date I started drafting the answers? I began my application over Christmas, but I’m planning to finalise everything and hit submit on 05/03/2026, once I’ve had my accountant glance over the financial bits.

I’m asking because a couple of the questions refer back to specific dates and I don’t want to confuse things. For example, in Box 1 I’ve talked about May 2023, when after a panic attack during a fully booked Friday night at The Green Fork my GP, Dr. Patel at Newgate Medical Centre, diagnosed me with generalised anxiety disorder and started me on 50 mg sertraline. I’ve also mentioned the inheritance I used in March 2021 to keep our pay‑what‑you‑can meals going after Mum, Susan Cartwright, died at Bassetlaw District General in January 2020.

If you could confirm that the declaration date should simply be the day I press “submit” (which I’m aiming to do on 05 March 2026), I’ll make sure everything lines up.

Best wishes,

Emma Louise Cartwright
Owner‑Chef, The Green Fork Bistro

metadata.subject: Clarification on declaration date for application form

Ring‑fenced time to finish Food Drink Hero award application and sort the declaration section before the submission window closes next week.

To‑do during this slot:

- Double‑check all dates mentioned: Mum’s death (Jan 2020), the inheritance I used in March 2021 to plug the gap in the pay‑what‑you‑can scheme, the NatWest overdraft extension in November 2020, and the council tax arrears letter that landed in February 2021.

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Harper <daniel.harper@outwoodvalley.ac.uk>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 24 February 2026, 07:41
Subject: Don’t forget to sign & date that declaration

Morning Em,

You were half asleep when you left, so writing this down before my Year 9s descend.

You said you were going to finish that big Food Drink Hero application tonight after service. Remember the last page where you have to tick the honesty box and add your signature and the date? Please actually put the real date you sign it, not the date you started the form back in January.

I know you were talking about aiming to submit everything on 04/03/26 because you’ve got a quieter evening, but if it slips to later that week, just make sure the declaration date matches whenever you finally press the button. You don’t need another admin headache on top of supplier price rises.

Also, what you wrote about the panic attack in May 2023 and Dr. Patel diagnosing you with generalised anxiety disorder is powerful – it shows how you’ve changed things at The Green Fork (the 10‑minute breaks, no‑shouting rule, etc.). Same with you being honest about using your inheritance in March 2021 and the overdraft in November 2020 to keep the ‘Plot to Plate’ and pay‑what‑you‑can meals going.

Anyway, this is just me nagging. Put TODAY’S date when you sign the declaration, whatever day that ends up being.

Love,

D

artifacts.content:
Title: Submit Food Drink Hero form – FINAL deadline
Date: Thursday, 5 March 2026
Time: 10:00–11:00
Location: Office nook at The Green Fork
Attendees: Emma Cartwright

Description:

Absolute last chance to submit the Food Drink Hero application before school run / lunch prep chaos.

Steps:
1. Log in to awards portal.
2. Quick skim of Box 1 answer (Food Drink Hero question – 600 words max). Check references to:
   • May 2023 panic attack during full Friday service, GAD diagnosis from Dr. Patel at Newgate Medical Centre, and starting sertraline 50 mg.
   • Using £4,800 from Mum’s inheritance in March 2021 after she died at Bassetlaw District General in Jan 2020 to plug the pay‑what‑you‑can shortfall.
   • Extending the NatWest overdraft by £12,000 in November 2020 and the council tax arrears letter from Feb 2021 when I prioritised paying growers.
3. Go to declaration section.
   • Tick all the “true and accurate” boxes.
   • Type full name: EMMA LOUISE CARTWRIGHT.
   • Enter TODAY’S DATE in the “declaration > date” field (should show as 05/03/2026 if I actually do this on time).
4. Screenshot confirmation page and email PDF to myself + Daniel.

No rescheduling this – if something explodes in the kitchen, delegate and still get the form in.

metadata.title: Submit Food Drink Hero form – FINAL deadline

artifacts.content:
From: Awards Team <entries@fooddrinkheroes.org>
To: Emma Cartwright <emma@greenforkbistro.co.uk>
Date: 6 March 2026, 11:03
Subject: Re: Clarification on declaration date

Dear Emma,

Thank you for your message and for submitting your completed Food Drink Hero application yesterday.

To confirm our policy for future reference: the “declaration > date” field should always reflect the calendar date on which you, the applicant, complete the declaration statement and submit the form. In your case, our system shows that you signed and submitted the application on 05/03/2026, which matches the date you entered in the declaration section, so everything is in order.

We appreciate the level of detail you provided in Box 1 regarding your journey with The Green Fork Bistro – including the context around the 2020–2021 community meals, the personal inheritance you used in March 2021 following your mother Susan’s passing in January 2020, and the NatWest overdraft extension and council tax arrears you navigated in early 2021. Your reflections on implementing mental‑health‑friendly systems in the kitchen after your May 2023 diagnosis of generalised anxiety disorder from Dr. Patel were also very powerful.

Our judging panel will begin reviewing entries from 10 March 2026, and we expect to notify shortlisted candidates by late April.

Kind regards,

Chloe
On behalf of the Food Drink Hero Awards Team
